title: Error 403 when authenticating Google OAuth credentials
symptom: When attempting to sign in with Google for OAuth in n8n, the user encounters a 403 error. This prevents the workflow from executing properly.
applies_when: Trying to authenticate with a Google account
not_this_if: The app is already verified by Google
The user is not using the same account for app creation and authentication
root_cause: The 403 error typically occurs when the Google app is not verified or the user is not listed as a test user in the OAuth consent screen. Google requires additional verification steps for apps that are not fully published, especially when using sensitive scopes.
fix_steps: Edit the Google OAuth credential in n8n to include your domain and callback URL.
Go to the OAuth consent screen in Google Cloud Console and add your email as a test user.
Save the changes and try authenticating again.
agent_instruction: Edit the Google OAuth credential to include your domain and add your email as a test user in the OAuth consent screen.
